message:
Last spring, my partner laughed at me for keeping a printed checklist taped inside our hall closet. 'Who even does that anymore?' Fast forward to a surprise ice storm warning at midnight—no time to think, no scrolling through old texts trying to remember what we needed. I grabbed that checklist, we knocked out the essentials in 20 minutes, and honestly? It felt like the easiest storm prep we've ever done.

Turns out that small bit of prep work—just having *something* written down and ready—takes so much of the mental scramble out of the moment. No second-guessing, no 'did we forget the flashlights?' Just calm and done.

Have you found a simple prep habit that's actually stuck around and made a difference for you?
